title: acalypha parvula var. cordifolia
intro: Acalypha parvula var. cordifolia, also known as the heart-leaf acalypha, is a plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its small, heart-shaped leaves and clusters of tiny, yellowish flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, while also exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
